title: Johnson & Johnson — Immunology — Thesis
html:
J&J Immunology Thesis Tracker

Last updated: 2026-04-26 Core question: Can TREMFYA overcome the Skyrizi-sized gap in GI while managing ICOTYDE cannibalization in derm — and make J&J the IL-23 franchise leader?

Thesis 1: TREMFYA Will Become a $10B+ Peak Asset — But Only If GI Delivers

The bet: TREMFYA's trajectory from $5B (FY2025) to $10B+ depends almost entirely on GI (UC + CD) uptake. Derm growth will plateau or erode as ICOTYDE captures oral-preferring patients.

Evidence FOR:

  • Q1 2026: $1.6B quarterly sales, beating $1.2B estimates — 74% YoY growth
  • Now the share leader for new patient starts in IBD (per J&J Q1 2026 earnings)
  • Broadest approved IL-23 portfolio: psoriasis + PsA + UC + CD — no competitor matches all four
  • IL-23 class penetration in UC went from 5% to 40% in one year — massive TAM expansion

Evidence AGAINST:

  • Skyrizi holds 75% capture rate among IL-23 drugs in frontline IBD (AbbVie data)
  • Skyrizi SEQUENCE head-to-head win in CD is a powerful promotional asset TREMFYA lacks
  • AbbVie projects Skyrizi at $17.3B in 2025 — 3x TREMFYA's run rate
  • Immunology segment declined 8.8% in Q1 2026 due to Stelara biosimilar erosion

Confidence: MODERATE-HIGH | Review: Q3 2026 (after two more quarters of IBD new-start data)

Thesis 2: ICOTYDE Will Cannibalize TREMFYA in Derm — And J&J Is Fine With It

The bet: ICOTYDE (oral IL-23, approved March 2026) will pull psoriasis patients from TREMFYA, but J&J retains the revenue within its portfolio. The net effect is positive because ICOTYDE expands the IL-23-eligible population to oral-preferring patients.

Evidence FOR:

  • ICOTYDE launched March 18, 2026 with "strong demand" from day one — J&J raised 2026 outlook by $300M
  • 70% IGA 0/1 at Week 16, adverse events within 1.1% of placebo — safety profile drives first-line positioning
  • CEO Duato: ICOTYDE has potential to become "one of J&J's largest products ever" ($5B+ peak projected)
  • Oral convenience eliminates injection hesitancy — expands the funnel of biologic-eligible psoriasis patients

Evidence AGAINST:

  • ICOTYDE PASI 90 (55% at Week 16) is numerically lower than injectable IL-23s (80%+) — efficacy gap may matter to dermatologists seeking maximum clearance
  • Currently psoriasis-only — if ICOTYDE never reaches GI/PsA, cross-brand messaging complexity increases
  • Bimzelx (60-70% PASI 100) and Skyrizi (Q12W convenience) remain strong injectable competitors in derm

Confidence: HIGH | Review: Q4 2026 (after 6 months of launch trajectory data)

Thesis 3: The Skyrizi SEQUENCE Data Gap Is TREMFYA's Biggest Vulnerability in GI

The bet: Without a head-to-head GI win, TREMFYA will lag Skyrizi in physician preference despite leading on new patient starts. The SEQUENCE narrative ("we beat Stelara in CD") gives Skyrizi a promotional moat that SOV alone can't overcome.

Evidence FOR:

  • Skyrizi SEQUENCE trial: superior to Stelara in Crohn's — the only head-to-head IL-23 win in GI
  • Skyrizi holds 75% capture rate in frontline IBD — suggests physician preference is already established
  • ZoomRx tracking data shows Skyrizi slightly outperforming TREMFYA on SDP effectiveness in GI (per knowledge doc)
  • No TREMFYA head-to-head GI trial announced or enrolled

Evidence AGAINST:

  • TREMFYA is the new-start share leader in IBD (Q1 2026 earnings) — execution can overcome data gaps
  • GI physicians increasingly view IL-23s as a class, not brand-by-brand — "IL-23 effect" benefits all
  • TREMFYA's Q8W dosing in GI is consistent with Q8W in derm — familiar for multi-TA practices
  • Stelara biosimilar pricing creates a "cheap predecessor" tier that makes head-to-head vs. Stelara less relevant

Confidence: MODERATE | Review: Q2 2026 (monitor new-start share trends post-SEQUENCE marketing)

Thesis 4: Stelara Biosimilar Erosion Is the Hidden Catalyst for TREMFYA

The bet: As Stelara loses revenue to biosimilars, it creates pull-through for TREMFYA in two ways: (1) J&J reps pivot from Stelara to TREMFYA messaging, and (2) physicians upgrading patients from biosimilar Stelara to p19-selective agents choose TREMFYA over Skyrizi when J&J relationship is strong.

Evidence FOR:

  • Immunology declined 8.8% in Q1 2026 (Stelara drag) — the worst is happening now, creating urgency to convert
  • Stelara was J&J's biggest drug ($10B+ in 2024) — the sales force capacity freed up is massive
  • Physicians with established J&J relationships may prefer TREMFYA over switching to AbbVie's Skyrizi
  • J&J's TREMFYA GI data in bio-experienced patients (including Stelara failures) provides a natural upgrade path

Evidence AGAINST:

  • Biosimilar Stelara is cheaper — cost-sensitive payers may prefer it over premium TREMFYA
  • Some physicians will trade down to biosimilar Stelara rather than up to any p19 agent
  • AbbVie's immunology sales force is the industry's largest — they'll compete aggressively for Stelara switchers

Confidence: MODERATE | Review: Q3 2026 (after 12 months of Stelara biosimilar market data)

Thesis 5: J&J MSL Engagement Leadership in GI Is a Durable Competitive Advantage

The bet: J&J's MSL team leads on SOV and reach in GI. This peer-to-peer engagement model builds physician confidence in TREMFYA specifically — not the IL-23 class generally — and converts to preferential prescribing.

Evidence FOR:

  • J&J MSLs dominate GI engagement with highest SOV and reach vs. competitors (per ZoomRx tracking)
  • GI is a relationship-driven specialty where MSL engagement directly influences formulary decisions
  • TREMFYA's multi-indication profile gives MSLs more conversation entry points vs. single-indication competitors

Evidence AGAINST:

  • ZoomRx tracking shows Skyrizi slightly outperforms on SDP effectiveness in GI despite TREMFYA's SOV lead — engagement quality may lag
  • AbbVie is investing aggressively in GI MSL expansion post-Humira
  • MSL impact is harder to measure and slower to convert than commercial rep activity

Confidence: MODERATE | Review: Ongoing (ZoomRx tracking data)

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home
